queries.description: Total revenue, payment count, and average payment amount by currency — helps understand the financial breakdown across different currencies
queries.sql:
-- Revenue and volume metrics broken down by currency
SELECT
  p.currency,
  COUNT(p.id) AS total_payments,
  SUM(p.amount) AS total_revenue,
  AVG(p.amount) AS avg_payment_amount,
  MIN(p.amount) AS min_payment,
  MAX(p.amount) AS max_payment
FROM payments p
GROUP BY p.currency
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

queries.description: Monthly payment volume and revenue trend — identifies growth or decline patterns over time
queries.sql:
-- Monthly aggregated payment trends
SELECT
  DATE_FORMAT(p.created_at, '%Y-%m') AS month,
  COUNT(p.id) AS total_payments,
  SUM(p.amount) AS total_revenue,
  AVG(p.amount) AS avg_payment_amount
FROM payments p
GROUP BY DATE_FORMAT(p.created_at, '%Y-%m')
ORDER BY month ASC

queries.description: Top 10 organisations by total payment revenue — highlights the highest-value customers
queries.sql:
-- Top 10 organisations ranked by total payment revenue
SELECT
  o.id AS organisation_id,
  o.parent_vertical,
  COUNT(p.id) AS total_payments,
  SUM(p.amount) AS total_revenue,
  AVG(p.amount) AS avg_payment_amount
FROM organisations o
JOIN mandates m ON m.organisation_id = o.id
JOIN payments p ON p.mandate_id = m.id
GROUP BY o.id, o.parent_vertical
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
LIMIT 10

queries.description: Payment volume and revenue by industry vertical — shows which sectors drive the most business
queries.sql:
-- Payment performance aggregated by parent vertical (industry sector)
SELECT
  o.parent_vertical,
  COUNT(DISTINCT o.id) AS organisation_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT m.id) AS mandate_count,
  COUNT(p.id) AS total_payments,
  SUM(p.amount) AS total_revenue,
  AVG(p.amount) AS avg_payment_amount
FROM organisations o
JOIN mandates m ON m.organisation_id = o.id
JOIN payments p ON p.mandate_id = m.id
GROUP BY o.parent_vertical
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

queries.description: Mandate activity summary by scheme — reveals which direct debit schemes are most actively used
queries.sql:
-- Mandate count and associated payment metrics grouped by scheme
SELECT
  m.scheme,
  COUNT(DISTINCT m.id) AS total_mandates,
  COUNT(p.id) AS total_payments,
  SUM(p.amount) AS total_revenue,
  AVG(p.amount) AS avg_payment_amount,
  COUNT(p.id) / COUNT(DISTINCT m.id) AS avg_payments_per_mandate
FROM mandates m
LEFT JOIN payments p ON p.mandate_id = m.id
GROUP BY m.scheme
ORDER BY total_mandates DESC

queries.description: Payment source performance comparison — determines which payment sources generate the most volume and revenue
queries.sql:
-- Revenue and volume metrics broken down by payment source
SELECT
  p.source,
  COUNT(p.id) AS total_payments,
  SUM(p.amount) AS total_revenue,
  AVG(p.amount) AS avg_payment_amount,
  MIN(p.charge_date) AS earliest_charge,
  MAX(p.charge_date) AS latest_charge
FROM payments p
GROUP BY p.source
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

queries.description: Average days between payment creation and charge date by vertical — measures collection lag across industry sectors
queries.sql:
-- Average lead time (days) between payment creation and charge date per vertical
SELECT
  o.parent_vertical,
  COUNT(p.id) AS total_payments,
  AVG(DATEDIFF(p.charge_date, p.created_at)) AS avg_days_to_charge,
  MIN(DATEDIFF(p.charge_date, p.created_at)) AS min_days_to_charge,
  MAX(DATEDIFF(p.charge_date, p.created_at)) AS max_days_to_charge
FROM payments p
JOIN mandates m ON p.mandate_id = m.id
JOIN organisations o ON m.organisation_id = o.id
WHERE p.charge_date IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY o.parent_vertical
ORDER BY avg_days_to_charge DESC

queries.description: New organisations and mandates onboarded per quarter — tracks growth in platform adoption over time
queries.sql:
-- Quarterly onboarding trend: new organisations and mandates created
SELECT
  CONCAT(YEAR(o.created_at), '-Q', QUARTER(o.created_at)) AS quarter,
  COUNT(DISTINCT o.id) AS new_organisations,
  COUNT(DISTINCT m.id) AS new_mandates
FROM organisations o
LEFT JOIN mandates m
  ON m.organisation_id = o.id
  AND YEAR(m.created_at) = YEAR(o.created_at)
  AND QUARTER(m.created_at) = QUARTER(o.created_at)
GROUP BY CONCAT(YEAR(o.created_at), '-Q', QUARTER(o.created_at))
ORDER BY quarter ASC
